models.generation_context: 1st-gen K4, introduced for 2025 MY as successor to Forte; sedan launched MY25, hatchback MY26
models.model_summary: Compact sedan that replaced the Kia Forte starting with the 2025 model year. Powered by a 147-hp 2.0L I-4 with IVT on most trims, with a 190-hp 1.6L turbo I-4 and 8-speed automatic on the top GT-Line Turbo. Class-leading rear legroom and a standard 12.3-inch infotainment screen across all trims. Earned 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+.

reliability.summary: JD Power VDS measures 3-year-old vehicles and has not yet rated the 1st-gen K4 (introduced MY25). Consumer Reports has no published predicted-reliability score for the 2026 K4. Kia brand finished 9th in 2025 JD Power VDS with 174 PP100 (slightly above 190 industry average), giving moderate proxy confidence at the brand level.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for the 2026 K4.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the K4's roomy cabin, generous standard-tech list (12.3-inch infotainment standard across all trims), and bold styling, calling it a smart compact-sedan pick on value. Critiques center on slow-responding 2.0L acceleration, vague steering, body roll in corners, and an underwhelming GT-Line Turbo that falls short of hot-hatch expectations. Edmunds rates it 7.8/10 overall.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the K4's styling, roomy interior, fuel economy, and feature-per-dollar value. Common complaints include reported electrical glitches, lethargy from the base 2.0L engine, and elevated road/wind noise at highway speeds.

notable_other: Standard Kia Drive Wise ADAS suite (FCA, LKA, SCC w/ Stop&Go, DAW)

trims.notes: Zero-to-60 estimated (~8.5-8.7 sec) based on Edmunds test data for similar 2.0L IVT compact sedans (147 hp). NHTSA had not posted a 2026 K4 rating at time of research. Wheel size and tire spec from spec sheet.

notable_other: Adds Blind-Spot Collision-Avoidance Assist w/ Parallel Exit

Adds Rear Cross-Traffic Collision-Avoidance Assist (RCCA)

Upgraded 6-speaker audio (vs. 4-speaker)

trims.notes: Same powertrain/fuel-economy/dimensions/wheels as LX. ADAS BCA/RCCA added as standard.

notable_other: SynTex (synthetic leather) seat trim — new for 2026

changes: Adds SynTex (synthetic-leather) seat trim
Adds heated front seats
Adds wireless phone charging
Adds BSC and RCCA (same as LXS)
17-inch wheels (vs. 16-inch on LX/LXS)

trims.notes: Same 2.0L powertrain as LX/LXS. Ventilated seats remain optional, not standard. 17-inch wheels per spec sheet.

notable_other: GT-Line sport styling: unique bumpers, gloss-black accents, 18-in wheels
10-way power driver seat with lumbar support standard
GT-Line Premium Package adds 12.3-in cluster display, wireless charging upgrade, Harman Kardon audio (optional)

Adds 10-way power driver seat with lumbar
Adds SynTex seat trim and heated front seats
Adds wireless phone charging
Optional sunroof, ventilated seats, heated steering wheel, 12.3-in cluster

trims.notes: Same 147-hp 2.0L IVT powertrain as LX/LXS/EX. Sport styling and equipment upgrades but no engine change. 12.3-in driver cluster and Harman/Kardon audio remain optional on GT-Line (standard on GT-Line Turbo).

notable_other: Memory driver seat + heated steering wheel standard

changes: Upgrades to 1.6L turbo I-4 (190 hp / 195 lb-ft) with 8-speed automatic — vs. 147-hp 2.0L IVT base
Adds standard panoramic power sunroof
Adds Harman/Kardon 8-speaker premium audio with subwoofer
Adds ventilated front seats and heated steering wheel
Adds memory driver seat with 10-way power adjust
Adds 12.3-inch TFT driver cluster (vs. 4.2-in on lower trims)
18-inch wheels (vs. 16-inch on LX/LXS)

trims.notes: Edmunds tested 0-60 in 7.5 sec for the 1.6T K4; manufacturer claim slightly faster. EPA combined 28 mpg (city 26 / hwy 33) verified at fueleconomy.gov ID 50059.

models.notes: K4 sedan is the Forte's direct replacement; introduced as the 2025 model year, and now in its second sales year for 2026. K4 Hatchback is treated as a separate model entry (kia.com markets it on its own /k4-hatchback page). All sedan trims share dimensions and chassis; GT-Line Turbo is the only trim with the 1.6L turbo + 8-speed AT — all others use the 147-hp 2.0L NA + IVT. IIHS rated 2026 K4 sedan TSP+ (small overlap front Good, moderate overlap Good, side Good, FCP veh-to-veh Acceptable, FCP pedestrian Good).

models.generation_context: 1st-gen K4 hatchback body style — new for 2026 alongside the K4 sedan introduced for 2025 MY
models.model_summary: Compact 5-door hatchback derivative of the Kia K4 sedan, new for the 2026 model year. Shares the K4 sedan's 147-hp 2.0L I-4 and IVT on EX and GT-Line, with the 190-hp 1.6L turbo and 8-speed automatic on GT-Line Turbo. Trades the sedan's trunk for 22.2 cu ft behind the rear seats (~59.3 cu ft seats folded). Skips the LX/LXS trims offered on the sedan; the hatchback starts at the EX trim.

reliability.summary: All-new body style on the K4 platform (introduced MY26); no JD Power VDS history. Brand-level Kia VDS 2025 = 174 PP100. Reliability proxies inherit from K4 sedan.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for the all-new K4 Hatchback.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers welcome the K4 Hatchback's return of an affordable hatch body style with substantially more cargo (22.2 cu ft) than the sedan, plus the same well-equipped cabin and 12.3-inch standard infotainment. Critiques carry over from the sedan: lazy 2.0L acceleration and modest driving dynamics even on the GT-Line Turbo. Edmunds calls it a smart small-car pick that's not a hot hatch.

owner_reviews.summary: All-new body style for 2026 with limited owner data published as of May 2026. Edmunds and KBB owner-review pages exist but report fewer than 5 reviews.

notable_other: SynTex synthetic-leather seats and heated front seats standard (hatch starts higher than sedan LX)
16 standard ADAS features including BCA and SCC with Stop&Go

trims.notes: Hatchback skips LX/LXS — starts at EX (similar equipment to K4 sedan EX, plus the extra cargo volume). 0-60 estimated from Kia 0-100 km/h claim of 9.8 sec → ~9.4-9.5 sec to 60 mph for 2.0L IVT. IIHS has not separately rated the K4 Hatchback for 2026 — only the K4 sedan earned TSP+.

notable_other: GT-Line sport styling on hatchback body
10-way power driver seat with lumbar support
Sunroof and ventilated seats available via packages

changes: Adds GT-Line sport styling and 18-inch alloy wheels (vs. 17-inch on EX)
Adds 10-way power driver seat with lumbar
Optional sunroof, ventilated seats, heated steering wheel via GT-Line Premium Package

trims.notes: Same 147-hp 2.0L IVT powertrain as EX. 18-inch wheels with 235/40R18 sport tires.

notable_other: Memory driver seat + heated steering wheel standard

changes: Upgrades to 1.6L turbo I-4 (190 hp / 195 lb-ft) with 8-speed automatic — vs. 147-hp 2.0L IVT base
Adds standard panoramic power sunroof
Adds Harman/Kardon 8-speaker premium audio
Adds ventilated front seats and heated steering wheel
Adds memory driver seat with 10-way power adjust
Adds 12.3-inch TFT driver cluster (vs. 4.2-in on EX/GT-Line)
18-inch wheels (vs. 17-inch on EX)

trims.notes: Edmunds tested 0-60 in 7.6 sec for the hatchback 1.6T. EPA combined 28 mpg verified at fueleconomy.gov ID 50073.

models.notes: K4 Hatchback shares chassis and powertrains with the K4 sedan but uses a 174.4-in length (vs. 185.4-in sedan) and 22.2 cu ft cargo behind the rear seats (or 59.3 cu ft with the seats folded). All trims FWD-only. Hatchback skips LX/LXS — starts at EX, so the hatch lineup is just 3 trims. IIHS has not yet rated the K4 Hatchback separately (sedan earned TSP+ for 2026). MotorTrend 0-100 km/h claim of 8.1 sec for the turbo translates to ~7.6 sec 0-60 mph confirmed by Edmunds test.

models.generation_context: 3rd-gen Kia K5 (DL3); current gen launched MY21, mid-cycle refresh for MY24
models.model_summary: Midsize sedan offered with a 191-hp 2.5L NA I-4 and 8-speed automatic on LXS/GT-Line/EX, or a 290-hp 2.5L turbo I-4 with 8-speed wet dual-clutch on the GT. AWD available only on GT-Line. Standard 12.3-inch infotainment across all trims for 2026. Did not earn IIHS Top Safety Pick (Poor moderate overlap, Marginal updated side).

reliability.summary: No model-specific 2026 JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports predicted-reliability score published. Brand-level Kia VDS 2025 = 174 PP100 (9th of mass-market brands).

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for this 2026 Kia model.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the K5's sharp styling, strong value, and generous standard tech (12.3-inch infotainment), particularly when comparing against Camry, Accord, and Sonata. Critics call out a numb ride on lower trims and lack of a hybrid option. The GT's 290-hp turbo and wet DCT add genuine sporting credibility but at a price that bumps against more refined alternatives. Edmunds ranks the K5 4th of 5 midsize sedans for 2026.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the K5's value, looks, fuel economy (often exceeding EPA highway), and seat comfort. Complaints include occasional engine codes/recalls and some interior trim quality issues.

notable_other: Full Drive Wise suite: FCA, LKA, SCC w/ Stop&Go, BCA, RCCA, DAW

trims.notes: EPA fueleconomy.gov ID 49325 (K5 w/ Stop-Start, 26/37/30 MPG) used as primary LXS source. IIHS did not award TSP to 2026 K5 (Poor moderate overlap, Marginal updated side test). NHTSA 5-star overall (5 side, 4 front, 4 rollover). 0-60 7.6 sec per Edmunds testing of similar 191-hp K5.

notable_other: GT-Line sport exterior styling and dual-tip exhaust

changes: Adds GT-Line sport exterior styling
Adds 18-inch alloy wheels (vs. 16-inch on LXS)
Adds SynTex synthetic-leather seat trim
Adds wireless phone charging

trims.notes: Same NA 2.5L 191-hp powertrain as LXS. 18-inch wheels with 235/45R18 tires.

changes: Adds all-wheel drive (only K5 trim with AWD)
Adds GT-Line sport exterior styling
Adds 18-inch alloy wheels
Adds SynTex synthetic-leather seats
Curb weight rises ~233 lb vs. LXS due to AWD hardware
Fuel economy drops to 24/33/27 MPG (vs. 26/37/30 on FWD LXS)

trims.notes: Only K5 trim offered with AWD (NA 2.5L). Same engine as LXS but heavier curb weight and lower MPG. EPA ID 49323 (K5 AWD).

notable_other: Genuine leather seat trim with heated and ventilated front seats
Memory driver seat + heated steering wheel standard

trims.notes: FWD-only. Same 191-hp 2.5L NA engine but slightly lower combined MPG (29 vs. 30) due to heavier curb weight. EPA ID 49324.

notable_other: 2.5T turbo I-4 producing 290 hp / 311 lb-ft (vs. 191 hp NA)
8-speed wet dual-clutch transmission with paddle shifters
Sport-tuned suspension and electronic limited-slip diff
SynTex sport seats with neon-green stitching

changes: Upgrades to 2.5L turbo I-4 (290 hp / 311 lb-ft) with 8-speed wet DCT — vs. 191-hp NA + standard 8AT
Quad-tip dual exhaust and sport-tuned suspension
Adds 19-inch wheels (vs. 16-inch on LXS)
Adds Bose 12-speaker premium audio
Adds panoramic sunroof
Adds 12.3-in curved full digital cluster
Adds heated/ventilated front seats, memory driver, heated wheel

trims.notes: GT is the K5 performance variant (290 hp 2.5T + DCT). Treated as a TRIM not a separate model — Kia markets it on the same kia.com/k5 page, not a dedicated showroom URL. EPA ID 49326 (DCT). 0-60 5.8 sec per Kia manufacturer claim.

models.notes: K5 lineup unchanged in structure for 2026: LXS, GT-Line FWD, GT-Line AWD, EX, GT. AWD only available on GT-Line. EX is priced higher than GT-Line AWD because EX adds significant equipment (12.3-in cluster, Bose 12-speaker, leather, panoramic roof, heated/vented seats, memory) — typical Kia luxury-trim positioning. GT uses a 2.5T turbo engine + wet DCT, all other trims share the 191-hp 2.5L NA + 8AT. Did NOT earn IIHS Top Safety Pick (Poor moderate overlap, Marginal updated side). 12.3-inch infotainment is now standard across all trims for 2026.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen Seltos, mid-cycle refresh for MY24; carried over for MY26
models.model_summary: Subcompact SUV positioned between the Soul (now discontinued) and Sportage. Powered by a 147-hp 2.0L NA I-4 with IVT on LX/S/EX, or a 190-hp 1.6L turbo I-4 with 8-speed automatic on the SX (AWD only). FWD standard on LX/S/EX with optional AWD; SX is AWD-only.

reliability.summary: No model-specific 2026 JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports predicted-reliability score published. Brand-level Kia VDS 2025 = 174 PP100.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for this 2026 Kia model.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers value the Seltos for its boxy practicality, generous standard tech, and competitive value among subcompact crossovers. Critics note that the base 2.0L IVT powertrain feels coarse and slow, and the SX turbo's payoff is undercut by mediocre handling. Highway crashworthiness ratings (Acceptable moderate overlap, Marginal updated side, Poor FCP vehicle-to-vehicle) keep it out of IIHS TSP for 2026.

owner_reviews.summary: Long-running model (Seltos has been on sale since MY21); 2026 carry-over has limited specifically-tagged 2026 owner data. Owners of recent Seltos generally praise cargo, value, and tech; complain about engine noise and limited acceleration on NA models.

notable_other: 8-inch touchscreen (LX is the only Seltos trim with the smaller screen)
Wired Apple CarPlay / Android Auto (wireless on higher trims)
Full Drive Wise suite including BCA, RCCA, SCC, LKA standard

trims.notes: fueleconomy.gov did not return a discrete FWD 2.0L entry on the 2026 Seltos browse page when this row was researched (AWD 2.0L is ID 49506); FWD numbers (28/34/31) sourced from kia.com manufacturer spec sheet and brand model-browse page per spec §4 EPA-unavailable fallback. AWD 2.0L Seltos = 27/31/29 (EPA ID 49506). Towing 2,000 lb properly equipped per Kia. 0-60 estimated ~9.4 sec for 147-hp 2.0L IVT FWD. NHTSA 5-star overall; IIHS scored Marginal updated side and Poor FCP vehicle-to-vehicle so no TSP for 2026.

notable_other: Upgrades to 10.25-inch touchscreen with native navigation (vs. 8-in on LX)
Adds wireless Apple CarPlay / Android Auto

Adds Bose 8-speaker premium audio with subwoofer

changes: Upgrades to 1.6L turbo I-4 with 8-speed AT (190 hp / 195 lb-ft vs. 147 hp NA + IVT)
AWD standard (SX is AWD-only)

trims.notes: AWD-only trim. EPA ID 49505 verified: 24/27/25 MPG. 0-60 ~7.8 sec per Kia 0-100 km/h claim 8.4 sec. Towing 2,000 lb.

models.notes: Seltos is a long-running model (since MY21, refreshed MY24). 2026 carry-over. Two powertrain options but all classified as 'ice' — single powertrain line per spec (no hybrid/PHEV/EV variants). All trims FWD-only except SX (AWD-only); S and EX available FWD or AWD. AWD requires DriveMode Select with Snow mode. Did NOT earn IIHS Top Safety Pick (Marginal updated side, Poor FCP vehicle-to-vehicle). Towing capped at 2,000 lb across the line.

models.generation_context: 5th-gen Sportage (NQ5), launched MY23; current MY26 carry-over
models.model_summary: Compact crossover with 187-hp 2.5L NA I-4 and 8-speed automatic in FWD or AWD across all gas trims. X-Line and X-Pro Prestige add rugged styling, AWD-standard, 1-inch lift, and 17-inch all-terrain wheels on X-Pro Prestige. Earned 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+ for vehicles built after May 2025.

reliability.summary: No model-specific 2026 JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports predicted-reliability score published. Brand-level Kia VDS 2025 = 174 PP100.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for this 2026 Kia model.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Sportage for its segment-leading cargo room, generous standard tech (12.3-in dual displays standard on EX+), and competitive value among compact crossovers. Critics note the NA 2.5L engine feels underpowered with AWD and that interior materials look better than they feel. Earned 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+ for vehicles built after May 2025 — one of nine models recognized.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the Sportage's cargo space, fuel economy, and exterior styling. Common critiques: NA 2.5L engine feels slow especially with AWD, mediocre touchscreen responsiveness on lower trims.

notable_other: Standard 12.3-inch touchscreen with wireless CarPlay/Android Auto
Standard Drive Wise suite (FCA, LKA, SCC w/ Stop&Go, BCA, RCCA, DAW)

trims.notes: IIHS TSP+ applies only to Sportage units built after May 2025. EPA ID 49455 = FWD 2.5L (25/33/28). AWD adds ~$2,000 and drops MPG to ~24/29/26 (EPA ID 49503). 0-60 estimated ~9.0 sec for 187-hp NA + 8AT. Towing 2,500 lb properly equipped.

notable_other: Adds Harman/Kardon premium audio w/ subwoofer
Adds 10-way power driver seat with lumbar
Adds dual-zone climate, heated front seats, heated steering wheel, wireless charging

Adds heated front seats and heated steering wheel

Adds wireless phone charging
18-inch wheels (vs. 17-inch on LX)
AWD available for +$1,800

trims.notes: Same NA 2.5L 187-hp powertrain. Available FWD/AWD.

trims.notes: X-Pro Prestige is the off-road-focused trim with all-terrain tires and slightly raised ride. EPA ID 49510 (Sportage X-pro): 23/26/24 MPG.

models.notes: Sportage gas lineup carries over for 2026 with no major changes. Single 187-hp NA 2.5L powertrain across all trims. FWD or AWD on LX/EX/SX/SX Prestige; AWD-only on X-Line and X-Pro Prestige. X-Pro Prestige is the off-road-focused trim (raised height + BFG all-terrains). EPA IDs 49455 (FWD), 49503 (AWD), 49510 (X-Pro). IIHS TSP+ applies to units built after May 2025. NHTSA 5-star overall.

models.generation_context: 5th-gen Sportage HEV variant, introduced MY23; current MY26 carry-over
models.model_summary: Hybrid variant of the Sportage with a 1.6L turbo I-4 + 64-hp PMSM electric motor producing 232 hp combined, 6-speed automatic transmission. LX is FWD-only; all other trims are AWD-standard. EPA-rated up to 42 mpg combined on FWD LX (best in segment among non-PHEV crossovers).

reliability.summary: No model-specific 2026 JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports predicted-reliability score published. Sportage Hybrid is the same powertrain as Hyundai Tucson Hybrid (shared platform), which has 3+ years of generally favorable owner reports.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for this 2026 Kia model.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers note the Sportage Hybrid delivers significantly better acceleration and fuel economy than the gas Sportage at modest price premium. 232 hp combined output and the 6AT make for a more refined experience than CVT-equipped rivals (Toyota RAV4 Hybrid). Earned 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+ for vehicles built after May 2025.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the Sportage Hybrid's combination of fuel economy, power, and standard tech. Critics call out a smaller 13.7-gallon fuel tank (vs. 14.3 on gas) and slightly noisier engine under hard acceleration.

notable_other: Hybrid Sportage LX is the only Sportage Hybrid trim with FWD
Standard 12.3-inch touchscreen with wireless CarPlay/Android Auto
Standard Drive Wise suite of ADAS features

trims.notes: EPA ID 49456 verified: 41/44/42 MPG combined (FWD). 1.49 kWh battery per Kia spec sheet. Hybrid LX is the only Sportage Hybrid trim offered with FWD; all other trims AWD-standard. IIHS TSP+ shared with gas Sportage.

changes: AWD standard (vs. FWD on LX)

models.notes: Sportage Hybrid uses 1.6T hybrid powertrain producing 232 hp combined (gas 177 hp + 64-hp PMSM motor). 1.49 kWh Li-ion pouch battery, 6-speed automatic. Sportage Hybrid LX is the ONLY hybrid trim with FWD; all higher trims (S/EX/X-Line/SX Prestige) are AWD-standard. EPA: 49456 (FWD 41/44/42), 49504 (AWD 35/36/35). IIHS TSP+ for vehicles built after May 2025 (same award as gas Sportage). Hybrid battery covered by 10yr/100k Kia EV/HEV warranty.

models.generation_context: 5th-gen Sportage PHEV variant, introduced MY23; current MY26 carry-over
models.model_summary: Plug-in hybrid Sportage with 268-hp combined system (1.6T + 97-hp PMSM motor), 13.8 kWh battery, 34-mile EPA all-electric range, AWD-standard. Available in just two trims: X-Line and X-Line Prestige. Earns up to 83 MPGe combined in charge-depleting mode and 36 MPG in charge-sustaining mode.

reliability.summary: No model-specific 2026 JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports predicted-reliability score published. Sportage PHEV shares its powertrain with Hyundai Tucson PHEV; aggregate reports suggest typical electrical-system PHEV concerns.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for this 2026 Kia model.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers see the Sportage PHEV as the most powerful Sportage (268 hp combined) and praise its 34-mile EV range for daily commute coverage with hybrid backup. Critics note the limited 2-trim lineup, no fast charging (Level-2 only, 2 hours), and high price premium over the Sportage Hybrid (which comes in 5 trims).

owner_reviews.summary: Limited owner data published for 2026 specifically. PHEV owners generally praise the combination of EV range for short trips and hybrid efficiency for longer drives; some criticize the slow Level-2 charging and limited DC fast-charge option.

notable_other: X-Line rugged styling (raised height, roof rails)

trims.notes: Charge-sustaining fuel economy 36 MPG combined (gas mode), charge-depleting 83 MPGe combined per spec §3.6 v1.1. Per spec §3.6: fuel_economy.city/highway/combined hold charge-sustaining MPG; ev_specifics.mpge_combined holds charge-depleting MPGe. EPA ID 49767 verified. 34-mile EPA all-electric range. Level-2 charge ~2 hours from 0-100%.

changes: Adds 19-inch wheels (vs. 18-in on X-Line)
Adds head-up display, Highway Driving Assist 2, surround-view monitor
Adds genuine leather seats with heated rear and ventilated front seats
Adds panoramic sunroof
Adds memory driver seat

trims.notes: Same PHEV powertrain as X-Line. Equipment matches gas/hybrid SX Prestige top trims.

models.notes: Sportage PHEV uses 1.6T + 97-hp PMSM motor for 268 hp combined system output, AWD-standard. 13.8 kWh Li-ion battery, 34-mile EPA electric range, 470-mile total range, 83 MPGe combined (charge-depleting) per EPA ID 49767. Only 2 trims (X-Line, X-Line Prestige). IIHS TSP+ shared with Sportage gas/Hybrid for builds after May 2025. Level-2 only — no DC fast charge.

models.model_summary: Mid-size three-row SUV with two engines: 191-hp 2.5L NA I-4 + 8AT on LX/S, or 281-hp 2.5T I-4 + 8-speed wet DCT on EX/SX/X-Line/X-Pro. FWD or AWD on most trims; X-Line/X-Pro SX-P are AWD-standard. Seats 6 or 7 depending on trim (captain's chairs vs. bench in 2nd row). Earned 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+ for vehicles built after September 2025.

reliability.summary: No 2026 JD Power VDS or CR predicted-reliability published. Brand-level Kia VDS 2025 = 174 PP100.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for this 2026 Kia model.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Sorento's standard 3-row practicality at a lower price than larger 3-row SUVs (Telluride, Pilot), the 281-hp 2.5T turbo's strong power delivery, and the segment-leading IIHS TSP+ for builds after Sept 2025. Critics call out a cramped 3rd row, lower towing than rivals (3,500 lb on 2.5T vs. 5,000+ on V6 rivals), and the 2.5L NA's mediocre acceleration.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise interior space, standard tech, value, and X-Line/X-Pro off-road look. Common complaints: 3rd row tight for adults, 2.5L NA on lower trims feels slow with full load.

notable_other: 7-passenger seating with 2nd-row bench (8 passengers on some configs)
Standard 12.3-inch touchscreen and Drive Wise suite
Standard wireless Apple CarPlay / Android Auto

trims.notes: EPA ID 49460 (Sorento FWD NA 2.5L 22/29/25). IIHS TSP+ applies to vehicles built after September 2025. Towing capped at 2,000 lb on 2.5L NA. 0-60 estimated ~8.7 sec.

notable_other: SynTex seat trim with heated fronts

Adds wireless phone charging

AWD available for +$2,000 (S AWD = $37,290)

changes: Upgrades to 281-hp 2.5T + 8-speed wet DCT with AWD

Raised ride height with full off-road skid plates

Adds surround-view monitor and parking sensors

trims.notes: Off-road-focused trim with all-terrain tires. AWD-standard. Top of Sorento gas lineup.

models.notes: Sorento gas has 7 distinct trim configurations across the LX-S-EX-SX line plus X-Line and X-Pro rugged variants. Two engines: 191-hp 2.5L NA (LX/S) and 281-hp 2.5T (EX/SX/X-Line/X-Pro). 2.5T uses 8-speed wet DCT, NA uses standard 8AT. SX and X-Line/X-Pro Prestige seat 6 (captain's chairs); LX/S/EX seat 7. IIHS TSP+ for vehicles built after September 2025. EPA IDs: 49460 (FWD NA), 49508 (AWD NA), 49461 (FWD 2.5T), 49509 (AWD 2.5T).

models.model_summary: Hybrid 3-row variant with 1.6T + 59-hp PMSM motor, 227 hp combined system output, 6-speed automatic transmission. Available in EX (FWD or AWD) and SX Prestige (AWD only). EPA-rated 37 mpg combined on FWD (best in 3-row hybrid segment).

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for this 2026 Kia model.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers note the Sorento Hybrid offers the best fuel economy in the 3-row mid-size hybrid segment (37 MPG combined FWD) and slots between the gas Sorento and PHEV in price. Critics call out the limited 2-trim lineup compared to gas (8 trims), the standard 6AT (not Kia's typical 8-speed), and modest 227-hp combined output for a 3-row.

owner_reviews.summary: Limited 2026-specific owner data. Hybrid owners praise efficiency for a 3-row but want more trim options.

trims.notes: EPA ID 49457 (Sorento Hybrid FWD 37/36/37 MPG). EX comes very well-equipped because it's the base hybrid (Kia uses gas EX as benchmark — same equipment tier). AWD EX available for +$1,800 (40,690). IIHS TSP+ for builds after Sept 2025.

notable_other: Nappa leather seats with 2nd-row captain's chairs (6 passengers)

changes: AWD-standard with Terrain Mode
X-Line rugged exterior styling
20-inch wheels (vs. 17-in on EX)
Nappa leather seats with 2nd-row captain's chairs (6 vs. 7 passengers on EX)
Heated 2nd-row seats and ventilated front seats
Head-up display and Highway Driving Assist 2
Memory driver seat and surround-view monitor
Fuel economy drops to 32/35/34 MPG (vs. 37/36/37 on FWD EX)

trims.notes: EPA ID 49507 (Sorento Hybrid AWD 32/35/34). Top hybrid trim with full equipment matching gas X-Line SX Prestige.

models.notes: Sorento Hybrid has 2 trims (EX, X-Line SX Prestige AWD) plus EX AWD trim adding a 3rd config. 1.6T hybrid producing 227 hp combined / 258 lb-ft. 1.0 kWh Li-ion battery, 6AT. EX FWD only Sorento Hybrid trim with FWD; SX Prestige AWD-standard. IIHS TSP+ shared with gas Sorento for vehicles built after September 2025. EPA IDs: 49457 (FWD 37/36/37), 49507 (AWD 32/35/34).

models.model_summary: Plug-in hybrid 3-row Sorento with 1.6T + 91-hp PMSM motor producing 268 hp combined / 270 lb-ft. 14 kWh battery, 31-mile EPA all-electric range, AWD-standard. Earns 74 MPGe combined in charge-depleting mode and 33 MPG charge-sustaining. Only PHEV 3-row SUV under $55k in the segment.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for this 2026 Kia model.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers value the Sorento PHEV as one of the only sub-$55k 3-row plug-in SUVs on the US market, with 31-mile EV range and AWD standard. Critics note higher curb weight, only 6-speed transmission, and tepid 0-60 versus rivals like the Toyota Highlander Hybrid (non-PHEV).

owner_reviews.summary: Limited 2026 owner data. PHEV owners praise daily EV-only commute capability and AWD; complain of slow Level-2 charging (3.4 hours from 0-100%).

notable_other: 6-passenger seating with 2nd-row captain's chairs (PHEV EX uses captains)

trims.notes: EPA ID 49766 verified: 33 MPG combined charge-sustaining, 74 MPGe charge-depleting, 31-mile electric range, 440-mile total range. Charge-sustaining MPG goes in fuel_economy per spec §3.6 v1.1; charge-depleting MPGe in ev_specifics.mpge_combined. 14 kWh battery, ~3.4 hours Level-2 charge. AWD-standard.

notable_other: Nappa leather seats with heated 2nd-row captains
Ventilated front seats and memory driver
Head-up display and Highway Driving Assist 2

changes: Adds 20-inch X-Line alloy wheels (vs. 19-in on EX)
Adds Nappa leather with heated 2nd-row captain's chairs

Adds head-up display and Highway Driving Assist 2
Adds surround-view monitor and parking sensors

trims.notes: Top Sorento PHEV. Same powertrain as EX AWD. 20-inch wheels marginally reduce range vs. EX 19-inch.

models.notes: Sorento PHEV: 2 trims (EX AWD, X-Line SX Prestige AWD). 1.6T + 97-hp PMSM motor for 268 hp combined / 270 lb-ft. 14 kWh battery, 31-mile EPA electric range, 74 MPGe charge-depleting, 33 MPG charge-sustaining. AWD-standard. EPA ID 49766 verified. Level-2 charging only (~3.4 hours). IIHS TSP+ shared with gas Sorento for vehicles built after September 2025.

models.model_summary: Three-row minivan with 287-hp 3.5L V6 + 8-speed automatic, FWD-only. Seats 8 on most trims; SX Prestige has VIP Lounge captain's chairs (7 seats) with power leg extensions and wing-out headrests. Did NOT earn IIHS TSP (Marginal updated moderate overlap, Poor updated side). NHTSA 4-star overall.

reliability.summary: No 2026 model-specific reliability score published. Kia brand VDS 2025 = 174 PP100.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for this 2026 Kia model.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Carnival's standout exterior styling, generous interior space, and class-leading luxury options like VIP Lounge captain's chairs. Critics note no AWD option (Toyota Sienna offers it standard) and the updated IIHS test downgrades for moderate-overlap and side impact, which dropped the Carnival from prior TSP status. Edmunds: 'Best-styled minivan for 2026.'

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise interior space, infotainment, and styling. Common complaints: no AWD available, transmission hunting on hills, fuel economy not class-leading.

notable_other: Standard 12.3-inch touchscreen with wireless CarPlay/Android Auto

trims.notes: EPA ID 49437 verified: 19/26/21 MPG. Towing 3,500 lb properly equipped. FWD-only (no AWD on any Carnival trim). IIHS: Marginal updated moderate overlap, Poor updated side — no TSP for 2026.

models.notes: Carnival gas lineup: LX, LXS, EX, SX, SX Prestige. 287-hp 3.5L V6 + 8AT, FWD-only. 8-passenger 2nd-row bench standard on LX/LXS/EX; SX/SX Prestige use 2nd-row captain's chairs (7 passengers). SX Prestige adds VIP Lounge seats. EPA ID 49437 (Carnival V6 19/26/21). IIHS no TSP for 2026 (Marginal moderate overlap, Poor side); NHTSA 4-star overall.

models.generation_context: 4th-gen Carnival HEV variant, new for MY25 — second model year for 2026
models.model_summary: All-new hybrid variant of the Carnival, introduced MY25 and carried over for 2026. Uses 1.6T + 74-hp PMSM motor for 242 hp combined / 271 lb-ft, 6-speed automatic, FWD-only. EPA 34/31/32 MPG combined (~52% better than V6 gas's 21 MPG). Same 4 trims as gas (LXS, EX, SX, SX Prestige) — no LX trim on hybrid.

reliability.summary: All-new hybrid variant introduced MY25; no JD Power VDS history. CR has not published a 2026 score.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for the Carnival Hybrid.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers welcome the Carnival Hybrid as the first hybrid offering in Kia's minivan, citing significantly better fuel economy than the V6 gas (32 vs. 21 MPG combined). The Sienna Hybrid still leads on segment fuel economy (35 combined), but the Carnival Hybrid wins on styling, infotainment, and luxury options like VIP Lounge seats. No AWD available remains a drawback vs. Sienna.

owner_reviews.summary: New variant for MY25 with limited 2026-specific owner reviews. Early owners praise the fuel economy gain and EV-mode operation at low speeds.

notable_other: Carnival Hybrid skips the LX trim (no LX hybrid)

trims.notes: Carnival Hybrid skips the LX trim; LXS is the base. 1.6T HEV system produces 242 hp combined. EPA Carnival Hybrid not yet listed as discrete IDs on fueleconomy.gov 2026 brand page (kiamedia spec sheet shows 34/31/32 MPG); spec.fuel_economy values sourced from kia manufacturer spec sheet with brand-make page cited per spec §4 EPA-unavailable fallback. IIHS not yet separately rated for hybrid variant; ratings inherited from gas Carnival (no TSP).

models.notes: All-new Carnival Hybrid introduced MY25; carried over for MY26 with 4 trims (LXS, EX, SX, SX Prestige) — skips the LX base offered on gas. 242-hp combined (1.6T + 74-hp PMSM motor), 6-speed automatic, FWD-only. 1.49 kWh Li-ion battery. EPA-rated 32 MPG combined per Kia spec sheet (EPA-published discrete entries not yet on fueleconomy.gov 2026 PowerSearch — brand-make page used as source per spec fallback rule). IIHS Carnival shared rating: no TSP for 2026 (gas applies to all body styles). Hybrid battery covered by 10yr/100k Kia warranty.

models.model_summary: Subcompact crossover hybrid (HEV-only for 2026 — PHEV discontinued, EV split as separate Niro EV model). 1.6L Atkinson + 43-hp PMSM motor producing 139 hp combined, 6-speed dual-clutch transmission, FWD-only. EPA up to 53 mpg combined on LX trim — class-leading among small-SUV hybrids. Kia markets the gas-Hybrid as just 'Niro' on its consumer site.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for this 2026 Kia model.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Niro Hybrid's class-leading 53 mpg combined (LX), versatile cargo space, and standard safety tech. Critics note modest 139-hp combined output (slow at highway merging), no AWD option, and that the SX Touring's price approaches Sportage Hybrid territory which offers more cargo and standard AWD.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise fuel economy and value. Common complaints: 6-speed DCT can feel hesitant, modest acceleration.

notable_other: 10.25-inch touchscreen standard with wireless CarPlay/Android Auto
FWD-only (no AWD on any Niro Hybrid trim)
EPA 53 MPG combined is class-leading among small SUVs

trims.notes: Kia markets the gas-hybrid Niro as just 'Niro' on the consumer site (the PHEV is discontinued and the EV is a separate Niro EV model). EPA ID 49459 is the FE-trim Niro at 53/54/53 MPG. EPA ID 49458 (standard Niro) is 53/45/49. Both share the same 1.6L Atkinson + 43-hp PMSM hybrid system. IIHS has not awarded TSP to the 2026 Niro. NHTSA had not rated 2026 Niro at research time.

Adds SynTex seats with heated fronts and 10-way power driver

trims.notes: Halo Niro Hybrid trim. Same powertrain throughout.

models.notes: 2026 Niro Hybrid is the sole gas-powered Niro variant (PHEV discontinued, EV is separate model). 1.6L Atkinson + 43-hp PMSM motor producing 139 hp combined / 195 lb-ft. 6-speed DCT, FWD-only. 1.32 kWh Li-ion battery. EPA IDs: 49459 (Niro FE 53/54/53), 49458 (Niro standard 53/45/49). The FE-trim is mapped to LX trim (smaller 16-inch wheels yield 53 combined); EX/SX/SX Touring use 18-inch wheels (49 combined). IIHS has not awarded TSP. NHTSA had not yet rated 2026 Niro at research time.

models.model_summary: Subcompact electric crossover with 150-kW (201-hp) PMSM front-motor, 64.8 kWh battery, 253-mile EPA range, FWD only. Two trims (Wind, Wave) with identical powertrain. CCS1 charging connector — does not include native NACS for 2026.

reliability.summary: No 2026 model-specific reliability score published. Niro EV is in its 3rd MY (since MY23) with limited owner-reported issues.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for this 2026 Kia model.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers note the Niro EV offers reasonable 253-mile range for the price and a tidy small footprint. Critics: 64.8 kWh battery is small vs. competition (Chevy Equinox EV, Volvo EX30), only 85 kW max DC fast charge (slow vs. 800V rivals like EV6), no NACS port for 2026 (still CCS1), and FWD-only.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise efficiency, hatchback practicality, smooth ride. Common complaints: slow DC fast charging, 400V architecture limits charging speed.

notable_other: Dual 10.25-in displays standard (vs. 10.25 + 4.2 on Niro Hybrid LX)
SynTex seats with heated front seats standard
Heated steering wheel and wireless charging standard
Optional Wind Preserve Package adds heat pump (+$1,300)

trims.notes: EPA ID 49664 verified: 126/101/113 MPGe, 253-mile range. MPGe values mirrored into fuel_economy.city/highway/combined per spec §3.6 v1.1. 64.8 kWh battery, 11 kW AC charge, ~85 kW peak DC fast charge (slow vs. 800V rivals). 400V architecture. CCS1 plug for 2026 (no native NACS upgrade for Niro EV).

notable_other: Adds ventilated front seats and memory driver
Optional Wave Preserve Package adds heat pump (+$1,300)

changes: Adds Harman/Kardon 8-speaker premium audio
Adds ventilated front seats and memory driver seat

models.notes: Niro EV: 2 trims (Wind, Wave) with shared 150-kW PMSM motor, 64.8 kWh battery, 253-mile EPA range, FWD-only. EPA ID 49664 verified for both trims (Kia lists single Niro EV entry — wheel/tire-set diffs minimal). MPGe values mirrored into fuel_economy.city/highway/combined per spec §3.6 v1.1. CCS1 charge port (Niro EV did NOT receive native NACS for 2026 — see EV6 for the brand's first NACS upgrade in MY27 plan, and EV9 retains CCS1 too). Heat pump available via Preserve Package on either trim. IIHS no TSP for 2026. NHTSA had not rated 2026 Niro EV at research time.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen EV6, launched MY22 — 800V architecture; carry-over for MY26 with native NACS port and $5,000 price reduction
models.model_summary: Compact 800V electric crossover. 2026 lineup: Light (Standard Range 63 kWh, Long Range 84 kWh), Wind, GT-Line — each available RWD or AWD. EPA range 237-319 mi depending on trim. Charges 10-80% in 18 min at 240+ kW DC fast chargers. Native NACS port for 2026 (first Kia model to receive factory NACS, alongside EV9). EV6 GT 641-hp performance variant DELAYED INDEFINITELY (not on sale).

reliability.summary: No 2026 model-specific reliability score. Kia brand VDS 2025 = 174 PP100; EV6 in its 5th MY since MY22 with mostly positive owner reports.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for this 2026 Kia model.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the EV6's 800V fast-charging (10-80% in 18 min), striking styling, and significantly reduced $37,900 entry MSRP for 2026 (~$5,000 lower than MY25). The native NACS port simplifies Tesla Supercharger access. Critics note: GT (641-hp performance variant) is delayed indefinitely; rear cargo trails Tesla Model Y; modest cargo behind the rear seats (24.4 cu ft).

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise quick charging, styling, and value. Common complaints: tight rear seat for tall passengers, limited cargo, slow software updates.

notable_other: Native NACS charge port (Tesla Supercharger direct access)
10-80% DC fast charge in ~18 min at 180+ kW
Standard heated front seats and heated steering wheel

trims.notes: EPA ID 50215 verified: 128/103/115 MPGe, 237-mile range. 63 kWh Standard Range battery. MPGe mirrored into fuel_economy per spec §3.6 v1.1. Native NACS port (first Kia w/ factory NACS, alongside EV9). Towing 2,300 lb properly equipped. IIHS no TSP for 2026; NHTSA not yet rated.

models.notes: EV6 lineup for 2026: 7 trims (Light SR RWD, Light LR RWD, Light LR AWD, Wind RWD, Wind AWD, GT-Line RWD, GT-Line AWD). $5,000 starting price reduction for 2026 ($37,900 vs ~$42,900 MY25). NATIVE NACS port for 2026 (Kia's first MY with factory NACS). EV6 GT (641-hp performance variant) DELAYED INDEFINITELY by Kia per Nov 2025 announcement — not on sale for 2026 and excluded from this catalog. EPA 5 IDs verified: Light SR (50215), Light LR RWD (50214), Light LR AWD 19in (50212), Light LR AWD 20in/GT-Line AWD (50213). 800V architecture, ~18 min 10-80% on 240+ kW chargers. Towing 2,300 lb properly equipped. IIHS no TSP for 2026.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen EV9, launched MY24 — Kia's first dedicated EV three-row SUV. MY26 adds native NACS charging port
models.model_summary: Three-row 800V electric SUV with 5 trims (Light, Light Long Range, Wind, Land, GT-Line AWD). 76.1 kWh SR or 99.8 kWh LR battery, 230-305 mi EPA range. Built at Kia's West Point, Georgia plant — qualifies for $7,500 federal tax credit. Native NACS port for 2026. EV9 GT (501-hp performance variant) announced but DELAYED INDEFINITELY — not on sale.

reliability.summary: No 2026 model-specific reliability score. EV9 in its 3rd MY (since MY24).

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for this 2026 Kia model.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the EV9 as one of the few three-row electric SUVs on sale, with class-leading interior space, distinctive styling, and 800V fast charging (10-80% in ~24 min at 350 kW). Earned 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+. Critics: Long Range AWD range only 283 mi vs. 300+ rivals; GT performance variant indefinitely delayed.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise spaciousness, 3rd-row usability, fast charging, and Tesla Supercharger access via native NACS. Common complaints: high curb weight impacting handling, limited range relative to battery size.

notable_other: 10-80% DC fast charge in ~24 min at 350 kW

trims.notes: EPA ID 49665 verified: 101/76/88 MPGe, 230-mile range. 76.1 kWh Standard Range battery (single rear motor, 160 kW). MPGe mirrored to fuel_economy per spec §3.6 v1.1. Native NACS for 2026. 2026 IIHS TSP+. Built at Kia West Point GA — qualifies for $7,500 federal EV tax credit.

trims.notes: Top EV9 trim sold for 2026. EPA ID 49668 verified: 92/72/82 MPGe, 280-mile range (21-in wheels). EV9 GT (501-hp performance variant) is a DIFFERENT trim ID 49669 listed on EPA but not on sale per Kia's Nov 2025 indefinite delay.

models.notes: EV9 lineup for 2026: 5 trims (Light RWD, Light Long Range RWD, Wind AWD, Land AWD, GT-Line AWD). Native NACS port new for 2026. Built at Kia West Point GA — qualifies for $7,500 federal EV tax credit. 800V architecture with 350 kW DC fast charge (10-80% in ~24 min). 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+ winner. EPA IDs verified: 49665 (Light SR), 49666 (Light LR), 49667 (Wind/Land LR AWD 19in), 49668 (GT-Line 21in). EV9 GT (501 hp, EPA ID 49669) was announced but DELAYED INDEFINITELY per Kia spokesperson Nov 2025 — excluded from 2026 catalog. Towing 5,000 lb with proper equipment.

notes: Phase 1 stubs created 2026-05-12. EXCLUDED from current US lineup: Soul (production ended Oct 2025, discontinued for MY26), Telluride (skipped MY26 — going directly to MY27 redesign), Forte (replaced by K4 starting MY25), Niro Plug-in Hybrid (discontinued for MY26 — only Hybrid and EV remain), EV6 GT (650-hp performance variant delayed indefinitely per Kia confirmation), EV9 GT (501-hp performance variant announced but not on sale as of May 2026 — Kia delayed indefinitely), EV3 (US-market 2027 MY only — kia.com page labeled 'Coming Soon'), EV5 (Canada exclusive in North America), Stinger (discontinued 2023). Performance variants K5 GT and Niro EV are TRIMS, not separate models (no separate kia.com model page). Sportage Hybrid/PHEV and Sorento Hybrid/PHEV split into separate model entries because Kia markets them on distinct kia.com pages (per spec §6.4).
